description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body that bulges outward at the center and tapers slightly toward the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three wide, weathered metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom. The wood surface shows a natural brown grain with some signs of aging and wear, while the metal bands are mottled with rust, giving the barrel a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The top is flat and appears to be made of the same wood as the sides, with no visible opening or spigot. The barrel is solid and robust, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban environments such as alleys, courtyards, or near garages. There are no visible handles, markings, or labels on the exterior.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops and a flat wooden top, showing signs of age and use. The surface is brown with metallic accents and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the flat wooden top and three rusted metal hoops, with the wood grain and aging clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the prominent metal bands, with the wood texture and rust details apparent.
description_view_2: Another angled view showing the consistent wood grain and the even spacing of the metal hoops around the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, emphasizing the uniformity of the wood and metal construction, with no additional features visible.
